topic: Someone receives flowers with no card attached
raw_output:
[story 1]

The box sat on the cluttered kitchen counter like an unwelcome intrusion. Liam stared at it, his jaw tight. A cascade of vibrant, almost offensively cheerful lilies and deep purple irises spilled out, their petals glossy and demanding attention. There was no note, just the sheer, unadulterated floral display. He picked up a stem, the silk cool against his palm, and immediately felt a tightening in his chest. Why? Who thought this was a good idea? He shoved the box toward the living room, intending to shove it further into the corner where it wouldn't interfere with his view of the television. He let out a low, frustrated sigh, the sound heavy with annoyance. He didn't want anything. He certainly didn't want anything delivered uninvited.

[story 2]

When Sarah found the package tucked beside her laptop, she frowned. The arrangement was stunning—a riot of sunflowers and deep crimson roses—but the absence of any accompanying message made her suspicious. She picked up the bouquet, the stems damp. "Seriously?" she muttered to herself, turning the flowers over in her hands. Her shoulders tensed, pulling inward as she processed the gesture. She didn't want attention. She wanted silence. She placed the flowers on the nearest bookshelf, a gesture of reluctant acceptance, her gaze fixed on the intricate pattern of the wallpaper, wishing the whole situation would just vanish.

[story 3]

Mark received the delivery while wrestling with a particularly stubborn spreadsheet. The sudden appearance of the large, brightly colored arrangement on his desk startled him. He blinked slowly, his eyes narrowed against the unexpected splash of color. He didn't move to inspect it; instead, he leaned back in his chair, crossing his arms tightly over his chest, his expression hardening into a mask of utter disinterest. "Great," he grumbled under his breath, the sound clipped and sharp. He picked up one of the blooms, wrinkling his nose slightly, and set it down without looking at the rest of the contents. He was already planning how to get it out of his sight.
